dashboard.label: Ping Monitor Setup
dashboard.description: Configure global settings for the Ping Monitor application

search.query: | inputlookup ping_monitor_settings_lookup | search _key="global" | head 1 | eval index=coalesce(index, "ping"), sourcetype=coalesce(sourcetype, "ping_monitor"), metrics_index=coalesce(metrics_index, "ping_metrics") | eval is_configured=if(configured=="true", "yes", "no")

div.p: Configure your Splunk index settings below. These settings are used by all dashboards and saved searches.
Check if your settings have been saved. If not configured, default values will be used.

search.query: | eval status=if(is_configured=="yes", "✅ Configured", "⚠️ Using Defaults") | eval last_updated_display=coalesce(last_updated, "Never") | table status, index, sourcetype, metrics_index, last_updated_display | rename status as "Status", index as "Events Index", sourcetype as "Sourcetype", metrics_index as "Metrics Index", last_updated_display as "Last Updated"

div.p: Enter your Splunk index and sourcetype values, then click Save Configuration to save.
💡 Instructions: Enter your settings below and click "Save Configuration". The settings will be stored in the KV Store and used by all Ping Monitor dashboards.

search.query: | makeresults | eval _key="global", index=if("$save_index$"!="" AND "$save_index$"!="$"."current_index"."$", "$save_index$", coalesce("$current_index$", "ping")), sourcetype=if("$save_sourcetype$"!="" AND "$save_sourcetype$"!="$"."current_sourcetype"."$", "$save_sourcetype$", coalesce("$current_sourcetype$", "ping_monitor")), metrics_index=if("$save_metrics_index$"!="" AND "$save_metrics_index$"!="$"."current_metrics_index"."$", "$save_metrics_index$", coalesce("$current_metrics_index$", "ping_metrics")), configured="true", last_updated=strftime(now(), "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S") | table _key, index, sourcetype, metrics_index, configured, last_updated | outputlookup ping_monitor_settings_lookup key_field=_key | eval result="✅ Configuration saved successfully at " . last_updated | eval details="Events Index: " . index . " | Sourcetype: " . sourcetype . " | Metrics Index: " . metrics_index | table result, details | rename result as "Status", details as "Saved Settings"

div.p: ⚠️ After Saving: Refresh this page to see the updated "Current Saved Settings" above. Other dashboards will automatically use the new settings on their next page load.
Check that Splunk can find ping data in your configured indexes. Uses the currently saved settings.

search.query: | inputlookup ping_monitor_settings_lookup | search _key="global" | head 1 | eval idx=coalesce(index,"ping"), st=coalesce(sourcetype,"ping_monitor") | map maxsearches=1 search="search index=\"$idx$\" sourcetype=\"$st$\" | stats count | eval count=if(isnull(count) OR count=0, 0, count)"

| inputlookup ping_monitor_settings_lookup | search _key="global" | head 1 | eval idx=coalesce(index,"ping"), st=coalesce(sourcetype,"ping_monitor") | map maxsearches=1 search="search index=\"$idx$\" sourcetype=\"$st$\" record_type=summary | stats count | eval count=if(isnull(count) OR count=0, 0, count)"

| inputlookup ping_monitor_settings_lookup | search _key="global" | head 1 | eval midx=coalesce(metrics_index,"ping_metrics") | map maxsearches=1 search="| mstats count(_value) as count WHERE index=\"$midx$\" metric_name=\"ping.*\" | eval count=if(isnull(count) OR count=0, 0, count)"

| inputlookup ping_monitor_settings_lookup | search _key="global" | head 1 | eval idx=coalesce(index,"ping"), st=coalesce(sourcetype,"ping_monitor") | map maxsearches=1 search="search index=\"$idx$\" sourcetype=\"$st$\" record_type=summary | stats dc(hostname) as count | eval count=if(isnull(count) OR count=0, 0, count)"

| inputlookup ping_monitor_settings_lookup | search _key="global" | head 1 | eval idx=coalesce(index,"ping"), st=coalesce(sourcetype,"ping_monitor") | map maxsearches=1 search="search index=\"$idx$\" sourcetype=\"$st$\" record_type=summary | head 5 | eval _time_str=strftime(_time, \"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S\") | table _time_str, hostname, target_ip, group, packet_loss_pct, avg_latency_ms" | rename _time_str as "Time", packet_loss_pct as "Packet Loss %", avg_latency_ms as "Avg Latency (ms)"

div.p: The health lookup allows you to correlate ping status with other Splunk data. Click the button to update it.

search.query: | inputlookup ping_health_lookup.csv | stats count as records, max(_time) as last_updated | eval status=if(records>0, "✅ Populated", "⚠️ Empty") | eval last_updated=if(isnotnull(last_updated), strftime(last_updated, "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S"), "Never") | table status, records, last_updated | rename status as "Status", records as "Records", last_updated as "Last Updated" | append [| makeresults | eval Status="⚠️ Not Yet Created", Records=0, "Last Updated"="Never"] | head 1

| inputlookup ping_monitor_settings_lookup | search _key="global" | head 1 | eval idx=coalesce(index,"ping"), st=coalesce(sourcetype,"ping_monitor"), action="$update_health$" | map maxsearches=1 search="search index=\"$idx$\" sourcetype=\"$st$\" record_type=summary | stats latest(packet_loss_pct) as packet_loss_pct, latest(avg_latency_ms) as avg_latency_ms, latest(target_ip) as target_ip, latest(group) as group, latest(entitytype) as entitytype by hostname | eval health_status=case(packet_loss_pct==0, \"Healthy\", packet_loss_pct>0 AND packet_loss_pct<50, \"Degraded\", packet_loss_pct>=50 AND packet_loss_pct<100, \"Critical\", packet_loss_pct==100, \"Down\", true(), \"Unknown\") | outputlookup ping_health_lookup.csv | stats count as updated_records" | eval result=if(updated_records>0, "✅ Updated ".updated_records." records", "⚠️ No data found") | table result | rename result as "Result"

div.p: Your Ping Monitor is now configured. Here is what to do next:

div.style: padding: 25px; background: linear-gradient(135deg, #1a2a3a 0%, #0d1520 100%); border-radius: 8px; border: 1px solid #2a4a6a;

td.p: Go to Ping Monitor Overview to see availability, latency, and health status for all monitored endpoints. Choose between Events, Metrics, or Combined view.
Use Asset Discovery to find other Splunk indexes that contain data about your monitored IP addresses and hostnames.
Visit Asset Health Correlation to analyze service impact and correlate ping health with other data sources using the lookup table.
Go to Settings - Saved Searches to enable alerts for endpoint down, high packet loss, and high latency conditions.

panel.title: 📖 Quick Reference: Using Health Data in Your Searches

div.p: Enrich any search with ping health status:
div.pre: index=your_index | lookup ping_health_lookup.csv hostname OUTPUT health_status, packet_loss_pct, avg_latency_ms | where health_status!="Healthy"
